info.title: Insights
info.description:
The API specification for Bandwidth's Insights platform.

## Base URL
`https://insights.bandwidth.com/api`

get.description: Returns a list of call events.

Retrieves an aggregated summary of call data based on the provided filters. This api is only available for accounts with Monitoring API feature.

The summary returned is broken down into time 'slices' depending on the time range requested.
The granularity is calculated based on:
 * 0-24 hours: hour
 * 25 hours - 7 days: day
 * 8 days - 30 days: week
 * 31+ days: month

Through the use of various query parameters, users can filter the call data by direction, phone numbers, sub-account, call result, SIP response codes, call type, hang-up source, and country.

Occasionally, underlying data may take longer than expected to load. To avoid connection timeouts, the API will return a `503 Service Unavailable` response in these cases, with an error message indicating that the data is still loading. If you receive a `503`, retry your request immediately with the exact same parameters.

Retrieves an aggregated summary of completed calls based on the provided filters. The call count is grouped by hangup source. This api is only available for accounts with Monitoring API feature.

 * 0-24 hours: hour

To avoid connection timeouts, the API will return a `503 Service Unavailable` response in these cases, with an error message indicating that the data is still loading. If you receive a `503`, retry your request immediately with the exact same parameters.

Get a history of created reports.

eq.description: Filter call data to only inbound or outbound calls.

gte.description: Greater than or equal to the specified date and time (start of range).

lte.description: Less than or equal to the specified date and time (end of range).

eq.description: Comma-separated list of phone numbers in E.164 format
Filter results by call completion status.
Comma-separated list of SIP response codes
Filter results by hang up source.
Comma-separated list of ISO A3 country codes
CRM ID of the account to get calls data from.

breakdown.description: The filter name to breakdown the report by.

description.example: Messaging details for particular snapshot date

filters.description: Use the Get Report Definitions endpoint to see the filters available for a report.

accountId.description: CRM ID of the account to get calls from.

startTime.description: Timestamp of when the call started, conforming to RFC 3339 format.

endTime.description: Timestamp of when the call ended, conforming to RFC 3339 format.

duration.description: Length of the call, measured in milliseconds.

callingNumber.description:
Phone number of the caller who initiated the call.

Format: E.164 with '+' prefix

calledNumber.description:
Phone number of the caller who received the call.

Format: E.164 with '+' prefix

sipResponseCode.description: Message generated by a user agent server (UAS) or SIP server to reply to a request generated by a client. A value of negative one (-1) indicates an unknown SIP response.

sipResponseDescription.description: A short description of the SIP response.

cost.description: Estimated cost associated with the call. This may not match directly to your invoice as this is an estimate. If this value is null it is because the cost has not yet been calculated. Cost is usually available within a day of call completion.

sourceIp.description: Source ip in ipv4 or ipv6 format.

destinationIp.description: Destination ip in ipv4 or ipv6 format.

customerJitter.description: Jitter experienced by the customer during the call, measured in milliseconds.

customerLatency.description: Latency experienced by the customer during the call, measured in milliseconds.

customerPacketLossPercentage.description: Packet loss percentage experienced by the customer during the call.

carrierJitter.description: Jitter experienced by the carrrier during the call, measured in milliseconds.

carrierLatency.description: Latency experienced by the carrier during the call, measured in milliseconds.

carrierPacketLossPercentage.description: Packet loss percentage experienced by the carrier during the call.

x5u.description: X5U URL of the certificate’s location.

postDialDelay.description: How long it takes for a calling party to hear a ringback tone after initiating a call, measured in milliseconds.

packetsSent.description: Count of packets sent during transmission.

packetsReceived.description: Count of packets received during transmission.

attestationIndicator.description:
STIR/SHAKEN attestation.
Filter Type: Exact Match, Multi Match.

Example:
  * Exact Match: A
  * Multi Match: A,B

x5u.description:
X5U URL of the certificate’s location.
Filter Type: Exact Match, Text Match

Example:
  * Exact Match: "https://example.com/x5u"
  * Text Match: "example"

countryCodeA3.description:
Filter Type: Exact Match, Multimatch.

Example:

* Exact Match: USA
* Multimatch: USA,IRL

Format: two-letter (A3) country code .

ipAddress.description:
Filter Type: Exact Match.

Example:

* Exact Match: 168.212.226.204
* Exact Match: 2001%3Adb8%3A3333%3A4444%3A5555%3A6666%3A7777%3A8888

Format: ipv4, ipv6 format ( : is url encoded as %3A)

qualityStatus.description: A combination of Jitter, Latency, and Packet Loss Percentage to determine the overall quality of the call. Quality Status filter is only available for accounts with Advanced Quality Metrics feature.

reportRegion.description: The region where the report will run. Defaults to the only available region. Required if multiple regions are available.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between this link and the resource.

description.description: A human-readable explanation that SHOULD be specific to this occurrence of the problem.
description.example: There was an issue with a field in your request body.

type.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem that SHOULD NOT change from occurrence to occurrence of the problem.

description.description: A human-readable explanation that SHOULD be specific to this occurrence of the problem.
description.example: There was an issue with a field in your request body.

monitorSliceDataMinutesOfUse.title: Monitor Slice Data - Minutes of Use

callRelease.description: SIP response code indicating the reason for call failure

callsLessThanOrEqualTo6Seconds.description: Total number of calls less than or equal to 6 seconds

callsLessThanOrEqualTo60Seconds.description: Total number of calls less than or equal to 60 seconds

callPercentageLessThanOrEqualTo6Seconds.description: Percentage of calls less than or equal to 6 seconds (0-100)

callPercentageLessThanOrEqualTo60Seconds.description: Percentage of calls less than or equal to 60 seconds (0-100)

monitorSliceDataNetworkEfficiencyRatio.title: Monitor Slice Data - Network Efficiency Ratio

monitorSliceDataCallsPerSecond.title: Monitor Slice Data - Calls Per Second

outboundCallCount.description: Number of outbound calls per second

inboundCallCount.description: Number of inbound calls per second

monitorResponseDataMinutesOfUse.title: Monitor Response Data - Minutes of Use

monitorResponseDataTotalShortCalls.title: Monitor Response Data - Total Short Calls

monitorResponseDataNetworkEfficiencyRati.title: Monitor Response Data - Network Efficiency Ratio

monitorResponseDataCallsPerSecond.title: Monitor Response Data - Calls Per Second

monitorDirection.name: direction
monitorDirection.description:
Filter call data to only inbound or outbound calls.
If not provided, both inbound and outbound calls are returned in the results.

monitorTimestamp.name: timestamp
monitorTimestamp.description:
Timestamp range filter for the time range to return records for.
Use `gte` for the beginning of the range (defaults to '7 days ago' if not specified).
Use `lte` for the end of the range (defaults to 'now' if not specified).
Values should include timezone, else UTC will be assumed.
Note, results will only be returned going back up to one year.
A larger timespan, difference between `gte` and `lte` will make the results less granular, for example a timespan of a few days, results will be hourly, whilst a timespan of months, result will be monthly.

monitorFromPhoneNumber.name: fromPhoneNumber
monitorFromPhoneNumber.description:
Filter results by originating phone number(s) in E.164 format (e.g. `+353123456`).
Accepts a comma-separated list of numbers.
**Warning:** Using this parameter may result in dramatically slower responses.
To improve performance, use additional query parameters to reduce the scope of your query.

monitorToPhoneNumber.name: toPhoneNumber
monitorToPhoneNumber.description:
Filter results by destination phone number(s) in E.164 format (e.g. `+353123456`).
Accepts a comma-separated list of numbers.
**Warning:** Using this parameter may result in dramatically slower responses.
To improve performance, use additional query parameters to reduce the scope of your query.

monitorSubAccount.name: subAccount
monitorSubAccount.description:
Filter results by Sub-Account ID.
The user making this API call must have access to the specified sub-account.

monitorCallResult.name: callResult
monitorCallResult.description:
Filter results by call completion status.
Accepts a single value: `COMPLETED` or `INCOMPLETE`.
If not provided, results for all call results will be returned.

monitorResponseCode.name: responseCode
monitorResponseCode.description:
List of SIP response codes to filter for.
These are three digit codes, i.e. regex pattern `\d{3}`.
If not set, then all response codes are included.

monitorCallType.name: callType
monitorCallType.description:
Filter results by call type.
Accepts a single value: `NATIONAL`, `INTERNATIONAL`, `EMERGENCY` Etc.
If not provided, results for all call types will be returned.

monitorHangUpSource.name: hangUpSource
monitorHangUpSource.description:
Filter results by hang up source.
Accepts a single value: `CALLED_PARTY` or `CALLING_PARTY`.
If not provided, results for all hang up sources will be returned.

monitorSourceCountryCodeA3.name: sourceCountryCodeA3
monitorSourceCountryCodeA3.description:
Filter results by source country.
Accepts a comma-separated list of ISO A3 country codes (e.g. `USA`, `CAN`).
If not provided, results for all source countries will be returned.

monitorDestinationCountryCodeA3.name: destinationCountryCodeA3
monitorDestinationCountryCodeA3.description:
Filter results by destination country.
Accepts a comma-separated list of ISO A3 country codes (e.g. `USA`, `CAN`).
If not provided, results for all destination countries will be returned.

monitorAccountId.name: accountId
monitorAccountId.description: CRM ID of the account to get calls data from.

accountId.name: accountId
accountId.description: CRM ID of the account to get calls from.

callId.name: callId
callId.description: Exact Match, any valid call ID.

callIdPath.name: callId
callIdPath.description: Filter Type: Exact Match, any valid call ID.

startTime.name: startTime
startTime.description:
Filter Type: Range using gt, gte, lt, and lte.

Note: If no startTime or endTime is specified, startTime will default to the last 24 hours.

endTime.name: endTime
endTime.description: Filter Type: Range using gt, gte, lt, and lte.

callingNumber.name: callingNumber
callingNumber.description:
Short number exact match, Short number Multimatch, Short number prefix match, E.164 exact match, E.164 Multimatch, E.164 prefix match, Alphanumeric exact match, Alphanumeric Multimatch, Alphanumeric prefix match.

Example:

* Short number exact match: 15555551234

* Short number Multimatch: 1555*,1234*,15555551234,15555554567

* Short number prefix match: 1555*

* E.164 exact match: +15555551234

* E.164 Multimatch: +1555*,+1234*,+15555551234,+15555554567

* E.164 prefix match: +1555*

* Alphanumeric exact match: WO1234567

* Alphanumeric Multimatch: WO1234567, PO4567891, WO*, PO*

* Alphanumeric prefix match: WO*

Alphanumeric string of length 1 to 15 characters with optional leading '+' (URL encoded as %2B) and optional trailing '*' (indicating prefix search)

calledNumber.name: calledNumber
calledNumber.description:
Short number exact match, Short number Multimatch, Short number prefix match, E.164 exact match, E.164 Multimatch, E.164 prefix match, Alphanumeric exact match, Alphanumeric Multimatch, Alphanumeric prefix match.

Example:

* Short number exact match: 15555551234

* Short number Multimatch: 1555*,1234*,15555551234,15555554567

* Short number prefix match: 1555*

* E.164 exact match: +15555551234

* E.164 Multimatch: +1555*,+1234*,+15555551234,+15555554567

* E.164 prefix match: +1555*

* Alphanumeric exact match: WO1234567

* Alphanumeric Multimatch: WO1234567, PO4567891, WO*, PO*

* Alphanumeric prefix match: WO*

Alphanumeric string of length 1 to 15 characters with optional leading '+' (URL encoded as %2B) and optional trailing '*' (indicating prefix search)

callDirection.name: callDirection
callDirection.description:
Direction of call.
Filter Type: Exact Match, Multi Match.

Example:
  * Exact Match: OUTBOUND

  * Multi Match: OUTBOUND-FORWARDED,OUTBOUND

callType.name: callType
callType.description: Filter Type: Exact Match, any valid call type.

callResult.name: callResult
callResult.description: Filter Type: Exact Match, any valid call result type.

hangUpSource.name: hangUpSource
hangUpSource.description: Filter Type: Exact Match, any valid hang up source.

sipResponseCode.name: sipResponseCode
sipResponseCode.description:
Filter Type: Exact Match, Multi Match.

Example:
  * Exact Match: 200

  * Multi Match: 200, 202

sort.name: sort
sort.description: The field and direction to sort by, combined with a colon. Direction is one of asc, desc.

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Return records starting at the nth record.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: The maximum records to return per page.

region.name: region
region.description: Filter Type: Exact Match, any valid region.

totalCount.description: The total count of objects returned in the response.

The origin server did not find a current representation for the target resource or is not willing to disclose that one exists.
POST is not an allowed method. Method must be GET
The user has sent too many requests in a given amount of time.
The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.
Query is still processing. Please try again.

errors.description: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an `Authorization` header that contains the word `Basic` followed by a space and a Base64-encoded string `username:password`.

- Example: `Authorization: Basic ZGVtbZpwQDU1dzByZA==`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to protected resources.

- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.

For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management)
